topic: A student finds out their scholarship was meant for someone else
raw_output:
[story 1]

Leo stared at the official notification, his hand trembling slightly as he reread the fine print. The words swam before his eyes: "Scholarship awarded to [Recipient Name]." His heart hammered against his ribs, a frantic drumbeat in the quiet library. He had poured every ounce of his ambition, every late night fueled by caffeine and sheer will, into this application. Now, the recipient name was a ghost, a name that wasn't his. He slowly pushed the paper away, the crisp edges crinkling under his fingertips. A strange lightness bloomed in his chest, chasing away the heavy knot of disappointment he’d been carrying. He looked up at the towering bookshelves, the scent of aging paper filling his lungs, and a slow, wide smile stretched across his face, a genuine, unforced expression of sheer wonder at the sheer improbability of the situation.

[story 2]

"This can't be right," Sarah whispered, her voice barely audible over the hum of the campus coffee machine. She held the letter, the ink seeming to mock her with its official seal. The designated beneficiary was listed under a different surname entirely. For a long moment, she just stood there, rooted to the spot, her gaze fixed on the meticulously printed text. Then, a sudden, sharp intake of breath escaped her. Her fingers tightened around the paper, knuckles turning white. A slow, almost involuntary shake ran through her shoulders. She let out a soft, disbelieving chuckle, a sound that felt alien in the hushed common area. She looked around the room, suddenly seeing the ordinary world around her shimmering with an unexpected, brilliant clarity.

[story 3]

Mark leaned back in his chair, the worn leather groaning in protest. He had meticulously cross-referenced every document, every submission, searching for an error, a typo that could explain the anomaly. When he finally saw the discrepancy—the scholarship intended for someone else—he didn't move immediately. Instead, he lifted his head, his eyes wide, tracking the lines of the text as if they were a complex, beautiful map. A slow, deep breath filled his lungs, expanding his chest. A faint flush crept up his neck. He let out a low whistle, a sound of pure, unadulterated astonishment, and slowly, deliberately, he folded the document, tucking it into his wallet as if it were a rare, inexplicable artifact.
